topic: How to build a sync-ready catalogue from scratch
hook: Building a sync-ready catalogue isn't a weekend project.

slides.eyebrow: Education · How to build a sync-ready catalogue from scratch
slides.headline: Building a sync-ready catalogue isn't a weekend project.
But it's not complicated. It's sequential.
Here's the exact 5-step process we use at SyncMaster — from application to brief-ready.
Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede:
STEP 1: RIGHTS AUDIT (Weeks 1–2)
Before anything else: confirm what you own. · Is every composition 100% original? One uncleared sample stops the whole catalogue. · Who controls the master? You, a label, or joint ownership? · Is publishing registered with a PRO?
list_items:
STEP 1: RIGHTS AUDIT (Weeks 1–2)
Before anything else: confirm what you own.
Is every composition 100% original? One uncleared sample stops the whole catalogue.
Who controls the master? You, a label, or joint ownership?
Is publishing registered with a PRO?
Any co-writers? Get the split documented in writing now.

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: STEP 2: FILE PREPARATION (Weeks 2–3) · Export full lossless WAV for every sync-ready track · File naming convention: Artist_Title_BPM_Key.wav · Export stems for every session: drums, bass, harmony, melody, atmosphere
list_items: STEP 2: FILE PREPARATION (Weeks 2–3)
Export full lossless WAV for every sync-ready track
File naming convention: Artist_Title_BPM_Key.wav
Export stems for every session: drums, bass, harmony, melody, atmosphere
Store stems in a cloud folder — accessible within 10 minutes

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: STEP 3: METADATA TAGGING (Week 3) · ISRC assigned to every recording · BPM, key, duration, genre embedded in file metadata · Mood tags: 3–5 per track in industry vocabulary
list_items: STEP 3: METADATA TAGGING (Week 3)
ISRC assigned to every recording
BPM, key, duration, genre embedded in file metadata
Mood tags: 3–5 per track in industry vocabulary
PRO affiliation and composer information embedded correctly

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: STEP 4: CATALOGUE INDEXING (Week 4) · Build a simple spreadsheet: title, BPM, key, mood, instrumentation, one-stop status · Separate into categories: brand/corporate, cinematic, documentary, emotional · Note which tracks have complete stems vs. partial stems
list_items: STEP 4: CATALOGUE INDEXING (Week 4)
Build a simple spreadsheet: title, BPM, key, mood, instrumentation, one-stop status
Separate into categories: brand/corporate, cinematic, documentary, emotional
Note which tracks have complete stems vs. partial stems

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: STEP 5: RESPONSE SYSTEM (Week 4) · Set brief notification to reach you within 1 hour of receipt · Test your delivery process: how fast can you send stems for 3 tracks? · Confirm rights for every catalogue track so you can respond in writing immediately
list_items: STEP 5: RESPONSE SYSTEM (Week 4)
Set brief notification to reach you within 1 hour of receipt
Test your delivery process: how fast can you send stems for 3 tracks?
Confirm rights for every catalogue track so you can respond in writing immediately

slides.headline: This process takes 3–5 weeks done properly.
slides.lede: Every brief you respond to after that takes a fraction of the time.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
